chapter_title: Part 3 – Managing misconduct under Division 10 of Part 2.4 of the ETRA
snippet: Procedures for an investigation and determination of an inquiry under Part 2.4 Division 10 of the ETRA generally involve the following steps: The department refers to these procedures as a misconduct procedure. Any misconduct procedure must be conducted in accordance with the principles of procedural fairness. This includes the right for the respondent to have a support person present at any meetings and be provided with any workplace adjustments that they require. Parties are committed to misconduct procedures being completed as early as possible within a period of 4 to 7 months, however it is recognised that circumstances may arise where this timeframe is not achievable. View Misconduct – Teaching Service in full screen View Misconduct – Teaching Service in table format Misconduct – Teaching Service Close Misconduct – Teaching Service Step 1 – Secretary’s delegate determines to commence misconduct investigation The Secretary’s delegate nominates an investigator to investigate the alleged conduct. The respondent will be notified, in writing by the delegate, of the investigation. The investigator conducts an investigation and establishes the allegations, particulars and alleged grounds for action (grounds for action refers to those under section 2.4.60 of Part 2.4, Division 10 (misconduct) of the Education and Training Reform Act 2006).
